title: contingency-dsl Experiment
description: Experiment and Paper wrapper nodes. An Experiment groups a single-schedule Program or a multi-phase PhaseSequence under a label (e.g., 'Experiment 1'). A Paper groups multiple Experiments as they appear in a single publication.

Experiment.description: A single experiment within a paper. Body is either a Program (single-schedule design) or a PhaseSequence (multi-phase design). Each Experiment has independent annotations — JEAB convention is to describe Subjects and Apparatus independently per experiment.

label.description: Experiment label as it appears in the paper. Examples: 'Experiment 1', 'Experiment 2a'.

Paper.description: A publication containing one or more experiments. Paper-level `shared_annotations` are inherited by every experiment; per-experiment annotations inside each Experiment.body override shared annotations by keyword (same scoping rule as PhaseSequence.shared_annotations vs per-Phase annotations). When no annotation is common across experiments, `shared_annotations` is empty and each Experiment body declares its own annotations independently.

shared_annotations.description: Annotations inherited by every experiment in the paper. A per-experiment annotation with the same keyword overrides the paper-level value for that experiment only.

Shaping.description: Within-session response shaping via differential reinforcement of successive approximations (Skinner, 1953; Catania, 2013). Present only in pre-expansion AST; resolved AST desugars to a single Phase whose schedule is determined by `method`. Distinct from `ProgressiveTraining` (across-session parametric progression). Surface keyword: `shaping`. Cross-references: Skinner, B. F. (1953). Science and Human Behavior. Macmillan; Galbicka, G. (1994). Shaping in the 21st century. JABA, 27(4), 739-760. https://doi.org/10.1901/jaba.1994.27-739.

label.description: Phase label for the shaping phase. Appears in PhaseSequence after desugaring.

target.description: Terminal response class name (required). Enforces Galbicka's (1994) Golden Rule 2: 'clearly define the terminal response.' Example: 'KeyPeck', 'LeverPress'.

method.description: Shaping method. `artful`: human-executed successive approximation (desugars to CRF + @procedure annotation + ExperimenterJudgment criterion). `percentile`: automated per Platt (1973) / Galbicka (1994) (desugars to Phase with Pctl schedule). `staged`: discrete pre-enumerated stages (desugars to ProgressiveTraining). Default `artful` reflects the JEAB convention where shaping details are left to experimenter judgment.

approximations.description: Ordered list of approximation labels (metadata only, not executed). Example for pigeon key-peck shaping: ['orient', 'approach', 'contact', 'peck']. Used by downstream tools for documentation and training protocol display.

dimension.description: Response dimension being shaped. Required when method=percentile; informational when method=artful. Maps to Pctl target for percentile shaping.

percentile_rank.description: Percentile rank for method=percentile. Required when method=percentile; must be integer in [0, 100].

percentile_window.description: Window size for method=percentile. Default 20 per Platt (1973) recommendation.

percentile_dir.description: Direction for method=percentile. See Pctl modifier in operant/stateful/grammar.ebnf §1.

stages.description: Ordered list of schedules for method=staged. Required when method=staged; desugars to ProgressiveTraining with one step per stage.

criterion.description: Phase-change criterion. Default for method=artful is ExperimenterJudgment. Required when method=percentile or method=staged.

phase_annotations.description: Additional annotations. The desugaring automatically injects @procedure("shape", target=..., method=..., approximations=[...]). User-supplied annotations are merged.

ProgressiveTraining.description: Syntactic sugar for across-session parameter progressions. Desugars to a sequence of Phase nodes (analogous to Repeat → Tand). Present only in pre-expansion AST; resolved AST contains the expanded Phase list. Surface keyword: `progressive`. Covers parametric sweeps (e.g., FI 2s→4s→8s, dose-response volumes) and conditions tables. Distinct from `Shaping` (within-session response shaping; Skinner, 1953). Reference: Sidman (1960), Tactics of Scientific Research; Zeiler (1977), Schedules of reinforcement: The controlling variables.

label.description: Base name for generated phases. Expansion appends _1, _2, ..., _n.

steps.description: One or more parameter variables with value lists. All lists must have identical length.

criterion.description: Shared phase-change criterion applied to every generated phase.

schedule_template.description: Schedule expression with {ident} placeholders referencing steps variables.

phase_annotations.description: Annotations shared across all generated phases. May contain {ident} placeholders.

interleave.description: Ordered list of phase-name references inserted between every pair of generated phases (gap block). Default semantics is intercalate: the gap block is also appended after the final generated phase. The last entry's no_trailing flag, if true, suppresses the trailing gap (intersperse). Each referenced phase becomes a template (does not appear standalone in PhaseSequence at its declaration position) and is deep-cloned at each insertion site with auto-generated label '<ref>_after_<progressive-label>_<i>'. See theory.md Definition 16 (Progressive Training Expansion with Interleave) and grammar.ebnf constraints 74-76, 63b.

InterleaveDecl.description: Single interleave directive within a ProgressiveTraining. Names a previously-declared phase (template) whose contents are deep-cloned at each interleave insertion site. The referenced phase must be declared earlier in the same experiment (no forward references — constraint 74). Self-references (target = enclosing or later ProgressiveTraining) are forbidden (constraint 75).

ref.description: Name of the phase to interleave. Must match a phase_label declared earlier in the same experiment.

no_trailing.description: If true, suppress the trailing gap block after the final generated phase (intersperse semantics). Only meaningful on the last InterleaveDecl in the array; if multiple entries set this true, the last occurrence wins.

StepsDecl.description: A named parameter variable with a list of literal values for ProgressiveTraining expansion.

name.description: Variable name referenced by {name} placeholders in the schedule template.

values.description: Finite literal list of parameter values. No computation; preserves non-Turing-completeness.

PhaseRef.description: Reference to a previously declared phase's schedule. Resolved during expansion by copying the referenced phase's schedule expression. Forward references are not permitted.

ref.description: Name of the referenced phase. Must match a phase_label declared earlier in the same experiment.
